message:
fix(frankenterm-web): harden attach state machine against mid-backoff transport closes and premature clean-close acceptance

The attach state machine had two edge-case bugs in TransportClosed handling:

1. A WebSocket close arriving while already in BackingOff state would
   clobber the pending retry timer and transition to Closed or trigger a
   redundant retry. This commonly happens when the browser fires a delayed
   close event after the state machine has already processed the timeout
   and entered backoff. The fix short-circuits TransportClosed in BackingOff,
   preserving the existing retry deadline.

2. An orderly close (code 1000/1001, clean=true) received while still in
   ConnectingTransport was incorrectly accepted as a terminal Closed state,
   preventing any retry. This occurs when a server rejects a connection
   immediately with a clean close. The fix gates orderly-close acceptance
   on the session having been Active before the close, so pre-Active clean
   closes schedule a retry instead.

Also adds unicode-width 0.2.2 dependency for terminal column width
calculations, getrandom wasm_js feature gate for WASM builds, and the
corresponding .cargo/config.toml rustflag for getrandom_backend="wasm_js".

Simplifies a redundant iter().any() pattern to contains() in wasm.rs.

Includes two new regression tests covering both scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -31,9 +31,11 @@ bitflags = "2.10.0"
 serde = { version = "1.0.227", features = ["derive"] }
 serde_json = "1.0.145"
 tracing = "0.1.41"
+unicode-width = "0.2.2"
 unicode-normalization = "0.1.24"
 
 [target.'cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")'.dependencies]
+getrandom = { version = "0.3.4", features = ["wasm_js"] }
 js-sys = "0.3.85"
 wasm-bindgen = "0.2.108"
 wasm-bindgen-futures = "0.4.58"
